description: Remove fictional `version` property from OpenTabsPlugin docs and fix LifecycleSequence illustration to include the missing teardown() hook.
qualityChecks: cd docs && npm run build && npm run type-check && npm run lint && npm run knip && npm run format:check

userStories.title: Remove ghost `version` property from plugin-class.mdx class signature and properties table
userStories.description: As a plugin developer, I want the SDK reference to accurately reflect the `OpenTabsPlugin` class so that I don't define properties that the platform ignores.
acceptanceCriteria: The class signature code block in sdk/plugin-class.mdx does NOT contain `abstract readonly version: string;`
The Abstract Properties table in sdk/plugin-class.mdx does NOT contain a row for `version`
The class signature still contains all real abstract properties: name, displayName, description, urlPatterns, tools
userStories.notes:
The `OpenTabsPlugin` class in `platform/plugin-sdk/src/index.ts` (lines 190-271) has these abstract properties: `name`, `description`, `urlPatterns`, `tools`, `displayName`, and abstract method `isReady()`. There is NO `version` property â€” the plugin version comes from `package.json`, not the class.

In `docs/content/docs/sdk/plugin-class.mdx`:
- Line 21: Remove `abstract readonly version: string;` from the class signature code block
- Remove the `version` row from the Abstract Properties table (the row with 'Semver version string')

Do NOT add any note about where the version comes from â€” that's covered by the plugin-development guide. Just silently remove the fictional property.

userStories.title: Remove `version` property from code examples in first-plugin.mdx, plugin-development.mdx, lifecycle-hooks.mdx, and resources-prompts.mdx
userStories.description: As a plugin developer, I want code examples to match the actual SDK class so that I don't copy-paste a property that does nothing.
acceptanceCriteria: The plugin class example in docs/content/docs/first-plugin.mdx does NOT contain `readonly version`
The plugin class example in docs/content/docs/guides/plugin-development.mdx does NOT contain `readonly version`
The full example in docs/content/docs/sdk/lifecycle-hooks.mdx does NOT contain `readonly version`
The TrackerPlugin example in docs/content/docs/guides/resources-prompts.mdx does NOT contain `readonly version`
The plugin-development.mdx field explanation list no longer includes a bullet for `version`
All modified pages have `lastUpdated` set to `2026-03-01`
userStories.notes:
Remove `readonly version = '...'` lines from plugin class examples in these files:

1. `docs/content/docs/first-plugin.mdx` line 62: Remove `readonly version = '0.0.1';`
2. `docs/content/docs/guides/plugin-development.mdx` line 83: Remove `readonly version = '0.0.1';` AND remove the bullet point at ~line 100 that says `**version** â€” plugin version string...`
3. `docs/content/docs/sdk/lifecycle-hooks.mdx` line 273: Remove `readonly version = '1.0.0';`
4. `docs/content/docs/guides/resources-prompts.mdx` line 497: Remove `readonly version = '1.0.0';`
5. `docs/content/docs/sdk/plugin-class.mdx` line 185: Remove `readonly version = '1.0.0';` from the Example section code block

In plugin-development.mdx, there is a field explanation list around lines 98-106. Remove the line about `version` (the one that says `**version** â€” plugin version string (e.g., semver "0.0.1"). Used for display purposes.`).

Do NOT change `lastUpdated` for pages that already have `2026-03-01`.

userStories.title: Add teardown() to LifecycleSequence illustration
userStories.description: As a plugin developer, I want the lifecycle timeline illustration to show all 6 lifecycle phases so the visual matches the text documentation.
acceptanceCriteria: The LifecycleSequence component in docs/components/illustrations.tsx shows 6 phases, not 5
The new 6th phase shows `teardown()` with description text `After onDeactivate, before re-injection (plugin update only)` and a `once` badge
The `teardown()` phase appears AFTER `onDeactivate()` in the timeline
The SVG viewBox height is increased to accommodate the new phase
The illustration maintains the same neo-brutalist style (solid box for one-time hook, 3px strokeWidth, drop shadow, var(--font-mono) text)
userStories.notes:
The LifecycleSequence illustration in `docs/components/illustrations.tsx` (starting at line 1773) currently shows 5 phases:
1. onActivate() â€” once, solid box
2. onNavigate(url) â€” repeats, dashed box
3. onToolInvocationStart(toolName) â€” repeats, dashed box
4. onToolInvocationEnd(toolName, success, durationMs) â€” repeats, dashed box
5. onDeactivate() â€” once, solid box

But the lifecycle-hooks.mdx docs describe 6 phases, with `teardown()` as a distinct phase after `onDeactivate()`. The illustration must add a 6th phase.

Implementation:
1. Increase the SVG viewBox height from 400 to ~490 (each phase takes ~86px of vertical space)
2. Add a new Phase 6 after onDeactivate, following the same pattern as the other solid-box one-time hooks
3. Use these values for the new phase:
   - Hook name: `teardown()`
   - Description: `After onDeactivate, before re-injection (plugin update only)`
   - Badge: `once` (solid badge, same as onActivate and onDeactivate)
   - Style: solid box (same as onActivate/onDeactivate â€” use primary fill opacity 0.12, 3px strokeWidth)
4. Add an arrow from phase 5 to phase 6, same as the existing arrows
5. Extend the central timeline line to reach the new phase

Follow the existing pattern exactly. The y-coordinate progression is: Phase 1 at y=8, Phase 2 at y=94, Phase 3 at y=180, Phase 4 at y=266, Phase 5 at y=352. Phase 6 should be at approximately y=438. Each timeline dot is centered vertically in its phase box.
